data.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend the PRECONDITIONS decisions_log entry schema with optional degradation fields, define the ack event format, and produce the consumer manifest.

**Why**: Foundation for all other stories in this epic. Defines the schema contracts that S2 (event writers), S3 (ack checker), and S5 (ack writer) all depend on. Without a frozen schema, format drift between stories causes silent pairing failures at story closure.

**Scope**:
- IN: Extend decisions_log schema with optional degradation:bool and degradation_type:string (values: inferred_decision | unresolved_question); null-coalescing semantics (legacy events without these fields treated as {degradation:false, degradation_type:null}); behavioral test against mixed legacy+new fixture enforcing null-coalescing; full ack event format definition (fields: decision_ids array, if_skipped string, timestamp ISO8601, sampled_set nullable array, schema_version); consumer manifest at plugins/dso/docs/precondition-degradation-consumers.md enumerating all PRECONDITIONS consumers (sprint validator, harvest-worktree attester, completion-verifier, sprint Step 10a checker) confirming absent-field handling for each
- OUT: Actually writing degradation events (S2); sprint closure check (S3); pre-commit hook (S4); ack CLI implementation (S5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, decisions_log entries in PRECONDITIONS events accept optional degradation:bool and degradation_type:string; consumers use null-coalescing reads treating absent fields as false/null
  ← Satisfies: "PRECONDITIONS schema...gains two optional fields on decisions_log entries" (SC1)
- When this story is complete, a behavioral test passes against a mixed fixture containing both legacy events (no degradation fields) and new events (with fields), confirming null-coalescing semantics
  ← Satisfies: "Behavioral test in the contract test suite that runs against a fixture containing legacy + new event shapes" (SC1)
- When this story is complete, the ack event format is FROZEN with fields: decision_ids (array of strings), if_skipped (string), timestamp (ISO8601), sampled_set (nullable array, populated only by sample-ack), schema_version (integer). No field additions, removals, or semantic changes are permitted by S3, S4, or S5 without re-opening this story
  ← Satisfies: foundation for SC5 and SC6 ack event contract consumed by 6068-cb2d
- When this story is complete, consumer manifest at plugins/dso/docs/precondition-degradation-consumers.md enumerates all PRECONDITIONS consumers and confirms each handles the absent degradation field correctly
  ← Satisfies: "Part C consumer manifest deliverable" (SC1)
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Depends on sibling epic c183-ed2a for decision_id format (session_id:content_hash); foundation schema must use the exact format specified there
- [Testing] Consumer manifest must explicitly enumerate: sprint validator (Step 10a), harvest-worktree attestation reader, completion-verifier, and any other reader that parses PRECONDITIONS events

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a planning infrastructure maintainer, PRECONDITIONS schema carries degradation fields with frozen ack event format so all consumers have a stable contract to implement against
